payload.answer: Required evaluation accounting fields for cost/latency reporting typically include: (1) token counts for input/prompt tokens, output/completion tokens, and total tokens; (2) provider ID and model ID (or equivalent identifiers); (3) estimated price (ideally with currency and whether it’s per-token or total, plus any calculation basis); and (4) latency statistics at least at p50, p90, and p95 (with units such as milliseconds).

entity_resolution.reason: No persistent entities were provided that warrant durable memory; this is a general explanation request.

payload.intent: Explain required evaluation accounting fields for context: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles (p50/p90/p95).

relationships.object: input tokens, output tokens, total tokens
relationships.subject: token accounting

memory_cards.statement: When evaluating an LLM-based system’s cost/latency performance, include accounting fields for: prompt/input tokens, output/completion tokens, total tokens, provider ID, model ID, estimated price (and currency/unit), and latency percentiles such as p50, p90, and p95.

details: Returned only non-deletion guidance suitable for evaluation accounting; no conflicting or superseded facts were asserted.

repair_options: If your fixture expects a specific schema, provide the exact field names and data types so the mapping can be precise.
If pricing is required, specify whether to report prompt/output token rates separately or only a combined total.
